headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 10, 2026
article:
**HEADLINE:** "Pirates' Shutout Win, Diamondbacks' Walk-Off Thriller Highlight Tuesday's MLB Action"

**Game 1: Pittsburgh Pirates @ Chicago Cubs**

The Pittsburgh Pirates took down the Chicago Cubs 2-0, behind a dominant performance from Shota Imanaga. The right-hander tossed 6.0 innings, striking out 9 batters without allowing an earned run. Bryan Reynolds led the Pirates' offense with a home run and 2 RBIs, while Ian Happ was the only Cubs player to record multiple hits. The Pirates' shutout win marked their first victory of the season.

**Game 2: Arizona Diamondbacks @ Philadelphia Phillies**

In a thrilling matchup, the Arizona Diamondbacks edged out the Philadelphia Phillies 5-4. Brandon Marsh's 3-run home run in the bottom of the 8th inning gave the Phillies a brief lead, but the Diamondbacks responded with a walk-off single in the top of the 9th. Bryce Harper recorded 2 hits for the Phillies, while Michael Soroka and Jesús Luzardo both had strong outings on the mound.

**Game 3: Miami Marlins @ Detroit Tigers**

The Detroit Tigers shut out the Miami Marlins 2-0, behind a strong performance from Keider Montero. The right-hander tossed 6.0 innings, striking out 7 batters without allowing an earned run. Javier Báez hit a solo home run for the Tigers, while Colt Keith recorded 2 hits for the Marlins.

**Game 4: Los Angeles Angels @ Cincinnati Reds**

The Los Angeles Angels routed the Cincinnati Reds 10-2, behind a strong team effort. Zach Neto, Jorge Soler, and Josh Lowe all hit home runs, while Yoán Moncada and Elly De La Cruz recorded 2 hits each.

**Game 5: Minnesota Twins @ Toronto Blue Jays**

The Toronto Blue Jays dominated the Minnesota Twins 10-4, behind a strong performance from their offense. Ryan Jeffers hit a 3-run home run, while Brooks Lee and Daulton Varsho also recorded home runs.

**Game 6: Athletics @ New York Mets**

The Athletics shut out the New York Mets 4-0, behind a strong performance from their pitching staff. Jacob Wilson recorded 3 hits for the Athletics, while Jeff McNeil had 2 hits for the Mets.

**Game 7: New York Yankees @ Tampa Bay Rays**

The Tampa Bay Rays edged out the New York Yankees 5-3, behind a strong performance from their offense. Yandy Díaz hit a 2-run home run, while Ben Rice and Amed Rosario also recorded hits.

**Game 8: San Francisco Giants @ Baltimore Orioles**

The San Francisco Giants took down the Baltimore Orioles 6-3, behind a strong performance from their offense. Willy Adames and Jung Hoo Lee both hit home runs, while Casey Schmitt recorded 3 hits.

**Game 9: Cleveland Guardians @ Atlanta Braves**

The Atlanta Braves dominated the Cleveland Guardians 11-5, behind a strong performance from their offense. Ronald Acuña Jr. hit a 2-run home run, while Kyle Manzardo and Angel Martínez also recorded hits.

**Game 10: Chicago White Sox @ Kansas City Royals**

The Kansas City Royals shut out the Chicago White Sox 2-0, behind a dominant performance from Kris Bubic. The left-hander tossed 7.0 innings, striking out 11 batters without allowing an earned run. Carter Jensen hit a solo home run for the Royals.

**Game 11: Washington Nationals @ Milwaukee Brewers**

The Washington Nationals took down the Milwaukee Brewers 7-3, behind a strong performance from their offense. Jake Bauers hit a 3-run home run, while James Wood recorded 4 hits.

**Game 12: Boston Red Sox @ St. Louis Cardinals**

The St. Louis Cardinals edged out the Boston Red Sox 3-2, behind a strong performance from their pitching staff. Wilyer Abreu and Jordan Walker both recorded 2 hits for the Red Sox.

**Game 13: Colorado Rockies @ San Diego Padres**

The San Diego Padres took down the Colorado Rockies 5-2, behind a strong performance from their offense. Gavin Sheets hit 2 home runs, while Luis Campusano also recorded a home run.

**Game 14: Houston Astros @ Seattle Mariners**

The Seattle Mariners dominated the Houston Astros 9-6, behind a strong performance from their offense. Yordan Alvarez hit a 3-run home run, while Christian Vázquez and Randy Arozarena also recorded hits.

**Game 15: Texas Rangers @ Los Angeles Dodgers**

The Los Angeles Dodgers edged out the Texas Rangers 8-7, behind a strong performance from their offense. Max Muncy hit 3 home runs, while Andy Pages and Corey Seager also recorded home runs.

**BIGGEST STORY OF THE DAY:**

The biggest story of the day was the Pittsburgh Pirates' shutout win over the Chicago Cubs, led by Shota Imanaga's dominant performance on the mound. The Pirates' victory marked their first win of the season, and it sets them up for a promising start to the year.
